title: Alex Sacerdote - How to Invest Through Technology Cycles - [Invest Like the Best, EP.477]
summary:
## TL;DR
This podcast features an interview with Alex Sacredote, founder of WhaleRock Capital Management, a technology-focused investment firm. Sacredote discusses his investment strategy, which focuses on technology S-curves, durable competitive advantages, and underappreciated earnings power. He also shares his thoughts on the current state of the AI market and his highest conviction position, Anthropic.

## Key Themes
* Alex Sacredote's investment framework: technology S-curves, durable competitive advantages, and underappreciated earnings power
* The current state of the AI market, including the emergence of Anthropic as a leading player
* The importance of understanding S-curves in investing, including identifying inflection points and measuring growth
* The role of intuition and anecdotal evidence in making investment decisions
* The challenges of investing in private companies, including getting allocation and building relationships with management teams

## VC Signals & Market Moves
* Alex Sacredote's firm, WhaleRock Capital Management, has invested in Anthropic, a leading AI company
* Sacredote believes that Anthropic has a strong competitive advantage and underappreciated earnings power
* He also mentions that his firm has invested in other private companies, including Stripe and Databricks
* Sacredote notes that the AI market is still in its early stages, with only 10 basis points of people really using AI

## Notable Quotes
* "When you have strategic inflection points, you can't trust the data. And strategic inflection points are about intuition, anecdotal evidence." — Alex Sacredote
* "The world doesn't think exponentially, and they're so focused on the next year, the next quarter. Very few people believe you can accurately predict two, three, four years out." — Alex Sacredote
* "We have an investment framework: S, competitive advantage, and then underappreciated earnings power. And when you get the right part of the S-curve, you get exponential unit growth." — Alex Sacredote

## Vocabulary & Concepts to Know
* S-curve: a mathematical model that describes the growth of a technology or market, characterized by a slow initial growth phase, followed by a rapid acceleration phase, and finally a slowing growth phase
* Technology S-curves: a framework for understanding the growth and adoption of new technologies
* Durable competitive advantage: a long-term advantage that a company has over its competitors, often due to its unique position, resources, or capabilities
* Underappreciated earnings power: the potential for a company to generate earnings that are higher than what is currently expected by the market.
